title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds version and immutable-retention recovery runbook, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of version and immutable-retention recovery runbook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
Apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 7528 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Versioning State Transition, keep Versioning State Transition connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 7528 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Versioning State Transition happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
State the Versioning State Transition preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Suspending versioning returns a bucket to its original unversioned behavior. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-restore-drill-evidence-1coi3g" that uses case 8375 to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
